title: Conjecture 2 (Krivelevich–Alon)
statement_text: There is some absolute constant $C > 0$ such that any bipartite graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ is $k$-choosable if $k \geq C \log \Delta$.
context_text: Krivelevich and the first author conjectured in 1998 that the upper bound ch$(K_{n,n}) \sim \log_2 n$ generalises: every bipartite graph with maximum degree $\Delta$ has choosability $O(\log \Delta)$. Johansson's result for triangle-free graphs gives only $k \geq C\Delta/\log\Delta$, and apart from constant factors no progress had been made before this paper.
paper_contribution: Proves Corollary 10: for any $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\Delta$ large enough, every bipartite graph with maximum degree at most $\Delta$ is $((1+\varepsilon)\Delta/\log^4\Delta,\,2)$- and $((1+\varepsilon)\Delta/\log\Delta,\,\log\Delta)$-choosable, constituting the first asymmetric progress toward the conjecture.
notes: PDF source — math notation verified readable; reference [2] in the paper.
paper_title: Asymmetric list sizes in bipartite graphs

title: Problem 3
statement_text: Given $\Delta_A$ and $\Delta_B$, what are optimal choices of $k_A \leq \Delta_A$ and $k_B \leq \Delta_B$ for which any bipartite graph $G = (V = A \cup B, E)$ with parts $A$ and $B$ having maximum degrees at most $\Delta_A$ and $\Delta_B$, respectively, is $(k_A, k_B)$-choosable?
context_text: The authors introduce $(k_A, k_B)$-choosability as an asymmetric generalisation of ordinary list colouring of bipartite graphs, where vertices in part $A$ receive lists of size $k_A$ and vertices in part $B$ receive lists of size $k_B$. Problem 3 asks for the optimal list-size pairs in terms of the one-sided maximum degrees $\Delta_A$ and $\Delta_B$, and subsumes Conjecture 2 as its symmetric special case.
notes: PDF source — math notation verified readable.
paper_title: Asymmetric list sizes in bipartite graphs

title: Conjecture 7
statement_text:
Let the positive integers $\Delta_A, \Delta_B, k_A, k_B$ satisfy one of the following.
(i) Given $\varepsilon > 0$, we have $\Delta_A, \Delta_B \geq \Delta_0$ for some $\Delta_0 = \Delta_0(\varepsilon)$, and $k_A \geq \Delta_A^\varepsilon$ and $k_B \geq \Delta_B^\varepsilon$.
(ii) For some absolute constant $C > 1$, $k_A \geq C \log \Delta_B$ and $k_B \geq C \log \Delta_A$.
(iii) $\Delta_A = \Delta_B = \Delta$, and, for some absolute constant $C > 0$, $k_B \geq C(\Delta/\log\Delta)^{1/k_A} \log\Delta$ or $k_A \geq C(\Delta/\log\Delta)^{1/k_B} \log\Delta$.
Then any bipartite graph $G = (V = A \cup B, E)$ with parts $A$ and $B$ having maximum degrees at most $\Delta_A$ and $\Delta_B$, respectively, is $(k_A, k_B)$-choosable.
context_text: Motivated by the asymptotic behaviour of $(k_A, k_B)$-choosability for complete bipartite graphs (Theorem 6 and Theorem 16), the authors conjecture three concrete asymmetric analogues of the Krivelevich–Alon conjecture. Condition (i) is weaker than Conjecture 2; conditions (ii) and (iii) are stronger. The paper shows Conjecture 7 holds for complete bipartite graphs (Theorem 15) and provides partial progress in the general case via Theorem 4 and its corollaries.
notes: PDF source — math notation verified readable. Three-part conjecture; each part is a separate asymmetric generalisation of Conjecture 2.
paper_title: Asymmetric list sizes in bipartite graphs
